summary_one_line: Input must be a well-formed sfnt/TrueType font with the literal 'GDEF' table tag at offset 0x0c so fallback_glyph_classes stays unset (false branch).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid sfnt font header (sfnt version 00 01 00 00, numTables fields) followed by the exact 4-byte table tag 'GDEF' (47 44 45 46) at offset 0x0c, so the font carries a real GDEF table and HarfBuzz does NOT synthesize fallback glyph classes — taking the false side of line 909.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with the 00 01 00 00 00 01 sfnt magic/header and carries the literal 'GDEF' tag at offsets 0x0c-0x0f; the BYTE DIFF shows W is fully concentrated 47(G)/44(D)/45(E)/46(F)x20 at those four offsets, supplying the GDEF table the parser needs to set fallback_glyph_classes=false.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds lack the 'GDEF' tag at 0x0c-0x0f (L bytes there are scattered low/zero values, never G/D/E/F), so HarfBuzz finds no GDEF table, leaves fallback_glyph_classes truthy, and falls into hb_synthesize_glyph_classes — the blocked true side (line 910).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the 4-byte table-tag comparison (sfnt tag == 'GDEF') and log the constant operand into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then substitutes the exact 47 44 45 46 bytes at the matching input offset, planting the magic tag that naive/value_profile can only reach by blind luck. This is input-to-state substitution.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of a check on c->plan->fallback_glyph_classes; the true side synthesizes glyph classes.
Taking the true side enters hb_synthesize_glyph_classes, which W never executes and L executes 381 times.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'GDEF' table tag, e.g. seed 1 has GDEF at offsets 0x0c-0x0f.
Across all winner seeds, offsets 0x0c-0x0f are fully concentrated on the bytes G/D/E/F (GDEF), while losers are scattered and never carry that tag.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes a CMP operand at the matching input offset.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile (the I2S-loser arm) has naive's havoc/token stack only with no I2SRandReplace.
Both decisive pairs differ only by the I2S technique and the winner resolves in 10/10 trials while the loser is blocked 10/10.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5727 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 60bd962a3dc60d31` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 4 and 5 of the ancestor chain, the floor signal that cmplog/vpc planted an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find planting the GDEF tag.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness gating the false branch on an exact 4-byte 'GDEF' tag comparison showed naive/value_profile resolving it as fast as cmplog/vpc, the I2S-substitution attribution would be refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: The mapping from the literal GDEF tag bytes to fallback_glyph_classes being unset is inferred from the font-table-parsing context, not directly observed at the compare site, so the exact CMP that I2S solves is assumed rather than shown.
